In [33]:
# Strandise the column names
df.columns = (
    df.columns
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(' ', '_')
    .str.replace('/', '_')
    .str.title()
)

RENAME_cols = {
    'Brid' : 'BRId',
    'Genderid' : 'GenderId',
    'Iaid' : 'IAID',
    'Amount_Of_Credit_Cards' : 'Num_Credit_Cards'
}
df.rename(columns=RENAME_cols, inplace=True)

# Parse date columns
df['Joined_Bank'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Joined_Bank'], errors='coerce')

df.head()

In [36]:
# Data Types
print('=== Data Types ===')
print(df.dtypes)
print()


# Missing Values
missing_values = df.isnull().sum()
missing_value_pct = ((missing_values / len(df))*100).round(2)
missing_df = pd.DataFrame({'Missing Count': missing_values, 'Missing Value %': missing_value_pct})
missing_df = missing_df[missing_df['Missing Count']>0].sort_values(by='Missing Value %', ascending=False)

print('=== Missing Values ===')
if missing_df.empty:
    print('✅ No missing values found.')
else:
    print(missing_df)

print()


# Duplicates
dupes = df.duplicated().sum()
print(f'=== Duplicates === {dupes}')

=== Data Types ===
Client_Id                              str
Name                                   str
Age                                  int64
Location_Id                          int64
Joined_Bank                 datetime64[us]
Banking_Contact                        str
Nationality                            str
Occupation                             str
Fee_Structure                          str
Loyalty_Classification                 str
Estimated_Income                   float64
Superannuation_Savings             float64
Num_Credit_Cards                     int64
Credit_Card_Balance                float64
Bank_Loans                         float64
Bank_Deposits                      float64
Checking_Accounts                  float64
Saving_Accounts                    float64
Foreign_Currency_Account           float64
Business_Lending                   float64
Properties_Owned                     int64
Risk_Weighting                       int64
BRId                                 int64
GenderId                             int64
IAID                                 int64
dtype: object

=== Missing Values ===
✅ No missing values found.

# Categorical
CAT_COLS = [
    'Nationality', 'Occupation', 'Fee_Structure',
    'Loyalty_Classification', 'GenderId', 'Location_Id'
]
# CAT_COLS = [col for col in CAT_COLS if col in df.columns]

for col in CAT_COLS:
    print(f'\n--- {col} ---')
    vc = df[col].value_counts(dropna=False)  #vc => value count
    pct = ((vc/len(df))*100).round(1)
    print(pd.DataFrame({'Count': vc, '%': pct}).head(10))

Univariate Analysis

In [39]:
# Distribution Plots for Numerical Columns
fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows = 4, ncols =3, figsize = (18,18))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(Num_COLS):
    ax = axes[i]
    data = df[col].dropna()
    sns.histplot(data, kde=True, ax=ax, color='steelblue', bins=30, edgecolor='white')
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_', ' '), fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('') 
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{x:,.0f}'))  #format x-axis labels with commas

    # Add mean and median lines
    ax.axvline(data.mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.3, label=f'Mean: {data.mean():,.0f}')
    ax.axvline(data.median(), color='orange', linestyle='-', linewidth=1.3, label=f'Median: {data.median():,.0f}')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)


# Hiding unused charts
for j in range(len(Num_COLS), len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)


# Adding Heading and Saving result
plt.suptitle('Distribution of Numerical Features', fontsize=17, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig('../reports/Results/01_numerical_distribution.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Saved ")

Saved
In [40]:
# Outlier Detection
fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=4, ncols=3, figsize=(18,18))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(Num_COLS):
    ax = axes[i]
    sns.boxplot(y=df[col].dropna(), ax=ax, color='lightcoral', flierprops=dict(marker='o', markersize=3))
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_', ' '), fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{x:,.0f}'))

# Hiding Unused Charts
for j in range(len(Num_COLS), len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)

# Adding Heading and Saving Result
plt.suptitle('Boxplots-Outlier Detection', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig('../reports/Results/02_boxplots_outlier.png', bbox_inches='tight')
In [41]:
# Age Distribution
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,4))
sns.histplot(df['Age'].dropna(), bins=20, kde=True, color='teal', edgecolor='white', ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Customer Age Distribution', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Age')

# Age group levels
age_bins = [18,30,45,60,80]
for b in age_bins:
    ax.axvline(b, color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=1)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/03_Customer_age_distribution')  # -> saving result
plt.show()

# Age group breakdown
age_labels=['Under 30', '30-45', '45-60', '60+']
df['Age_Group'] = pd.cut(df['Age'], bins=[0,30,45,60,120],
                         labels=age_labels)
print('\n Age Group Breakdown')
print(pd.DataFrame({
    'Age_Group': age_labels, 
    'Count': df['Age_Group'].value_counts().sort_index().values
}))

 Age Group Breakdown
  Age_Group  Count
0  Under 30    610
1     30-45    640
2     45-60    666
3       60+   1084
In [42]:
# Categorical Distribution

fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=3, ncols=2, figsize=(20, 18))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(CAT_COLS[:6]):
    ax = axes[i]
    counts = df[col].value_counts().head(10)
    if col == 'GenderId':
        counts.index = counts.index.map({1: 'Male', 2: 'Female'})
    sns.barplot(x=counts.values, y=counts.index, ax=ax,
                palette='viridis', orient='h')
    ax.set_title(col.replace('_',' '), fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Count')
    ax.set_ylabel('')
    for p in ax.patches:
        ax.annotate(f'{int(p.get_width()):,}',
                    (p.get_width()+0.5, p.get_y()+p.get_height()/2),
                    va='center', fontsize=9)
        

# Adding Heading and Saving Result
plt.suptitle('Categorical Feature Distributions', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig('../reports/Results/04_categorical_distributions.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Customer Tenure Analysis

In [43]:
# Deriving tenure
import datetime
TODAY = pd.Timestamp('today')

df['Tenure_Years'] = ((TODAY - df['Joined_Bank']).dt.days / 365.25).round(1) # 365.25-> Average day in a year 
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(14,5))


# Histogram of Tenure
sns.histplot(df['Tenure_Years'].dropna(), bins=20, kde=True,
             color='mediumseagreen', edgecolor='white', ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title('Customer Tenure Distribution (Years)', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Tenure (Years)')


# New Customer per year
df['Join_Year'] = df['Joined_Bank'].dt.year
yearly = df['Join_Year'].value_counts().sort_index()
axes[1].bar(yearly.index.astype(str), yearly.values, color='steelblue', edgecolor='white')
axes[1].set_title('New Customers Joined Per Year', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Year')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Count')
axes[1].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/05_tenure_analysis_png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

print(f"\n Average Tenure: {df['Tenure_Years'].mean():.1f} years")
print(f"Longest Customer Tenure: {df['Tenure_Years'].max():.1f}years")

 Average Tenure: 15.9 years
Longest Customer Tenure: 31.5years

Bivariate Analysis

In [44]:
# Income by Occupation (top 20)
top_occ = df['Occupation'].value_counts().head(20).index
plot_df = df[df['Occupation'].isin(top_occ)]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(20,8))
order = (
    plot_df.groupby('Occupation')['Estimated_Income'].median().sort_values(ascending=False).index
)
sns.boxplot(data=plot_df, x='Occupation', y='Estimated_Income', order=order,
            palette='Set2', ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Estimated Income by Top 20 Occupation', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Occupation')
ax.set_ylabel('Estimated Income')
ax.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=30)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'${x:,.0f}'))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/06_income_by_occupation.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [45]:
# Income by Loyalty Classification
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14,5))

# Median income as per loyalty tier
loyalty_income = df.groupby('Loyalty_Classification')['Estimated_Income'].median().sort_values(ascending=False)
axes[0].bar(loyalty_income.index, loyalty_income.values, color=sns.color_palette('coolwarm', len(loyalty_income)))
axes[0].set_title('Median Income by Loyalty Tier', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Median Income')
axes[0].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x,_: f'{x:,.0f}'))


# Customer Count per loyalty tier
loyalty_count = df['Loyalty_Classification'].value_counts()
axes[1].pie(loyalty_count.values, labels=loyalty_count.index, autopct='%1.1f%%',
            colors=sns.color_palette('pastel'), startangle=140)
axes[1].set_title('Customer Share by Loyalty Tier', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig('../reports/Results/07_loyalty_analysis.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/08_deposits_by_location.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Correlation Analysis

In [49]:
from scipy import stats
corr_matrix = df[Num_COLS].corr(method='spearman')

mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr_matrix, dtype=bool))  #upper traingle mask

fig, axis = plt.subplots(figsize=(22,10))
sns.heatmap(
    corr_matrix, mask=mask,
    annot=True, fmt='.2f', cmap='RdYlGn',
    center=0, vmin=-1, vmax=1,
    linewidths=0.5, linecolor='white',
    annot_kws={'size': 9},
    ax=axis
)
axis.set_title('Correlation Matrix - Numerical feature', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', pad=15)
plt.xticks(rotation=40, ha='right')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/11_correlation_heatmap.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Top correlation (excluding self-correlation)
corr_pairs = (
    corr_matrix.where(~mask)
    .stack()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={'level_0':'Feature_1', 'level_1':'Feature_2', 0:'Correlation'})
    .sort_values('Correlation', key=abs, ascending=False)
    .head(10)
)
print('\n Top 10 Correlated Features Pairs:')
print(corr_pairs.to_string(index=False))

 Top 10 Correlated Features Pairs:
             Feature_1           Feature_2  Correlation
     Checking_Accounts       Bank_Deposits         0.88
       Saving_Accounts       Bank_Deposits         0.82
       Saving_Accounts   Checking_Accounts         0.64
      Business_Lending       Bank_Deposits         0.42
Superannuation_Savings    Estimated_Income         0.37
      Business_Lending          Bank_Loans         0.36
         Bank_Deposits          Bank_Loans         0.36
         Bank_Deposits Credit_Card_Balance         0.35
      Business_Lending    Estimated_Income         0.33
      Business_Lending   Checking_Accounts         0.32

Feature Engineering(Deriving features)

In [50]:
# Total Assets 
df['Total_Assets'] = (
    df['Bank_Deposits'].fillna(0)
    +df['Superannuation_Savings'].fillna(0)
    +df['Properties_Owned'].fillna(0)*500_000       #rough property value proxy
)


# Total Liabilities
df['Total_Liabilities'] = (
    df['Credit_Card_Balance'].fillna(0)
    +df['Bank_Loans'].fillna(0)
    +df['Business_Lending'].fillna(0)
)


# Debt-to-income Ratio
df['Debt_to_Income'] = df['Total_Liabilities']/(df['Estimated_Income'].replace(0, np.nan))


# Account Diversity Score -> More score means more banking products, more enaged and more financially active
df['Account_Diversity'] = (
    df['Checking_Accounts'].fillna(0)
    +df['Saving_Accounts'].fillna(0)
    +df['Num_Credit_Cards'].fillna(0)
    +df['Foreign_Currency_Account'].fillna(0)
)


# Net Worth
df['Net_Worth'] = df['Total_Assets']-df['Total_Liabilities']

print('✅ New Feature Added:')
feature_added = ['Total_Assets', 'Total_Liabilities', 'Debt_to_Income', 'Account_Diversity', 'Net_Worth', 'Tenure_Years']
print(df[feature_added].describe().T[['mean','min','50%','max']].round(2))

✅ New Feature Added:
                          mean           min          50%          max
Total_Assets      1,456,425.13      2,150.05 1,384,812.63 5,276,374.25
Total_Liabilities 1,461,322.17     55,951.70 1,225,764.06 6,078,679.25
Debt_to_Income           11.62          0.39         8.66        93.52
Account_Diversity   583,886.30        955.04   470,466.69 2,624,005.14
Net_Worth            -4,897.04 -3,981,368.08    29,399.76 3,672,548.34
Tenure_Years             15.86          4.50        15.35        31.50
In [51]:
# Debt-to-Income Distribution

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14,5))

clean_dti = df['Debt_to_Income'].clip(0, 10).dropna()
sns.histplot(clean_dti, bins=30, kde=True, color='salmon', edgecolor='white', ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title('Debt-to-Income Ratio Distribution', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Debt-to-Income Ratio')
axes[0].axvline(1.0, color='red', linestyle='--', label='DTI = 1.0 (debt = income)')
axes[0].legend()


sns.histplot(df['Net_Worth'].clip(-500_000, 5_000_000).dropna(), bins=30,
             kde=True, color='mediumaquamarine', edgecolor='white', ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title('Net Worth Distribution (capped)', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Net Worth ($)')
axes[1].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'${x:,.0f}'))
axes[1].axvline(0, color='red', linestyle='--', label='Net Worth = 0')
axes[1].legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/12_added_features.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Risk Weighting Analysis

In [52]:
# Risk Weighting Analysis
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14,5))

# Overall Distribution
risk_counts = df['Risk_Weighting'].value_counts().sort_index()
axes[0].bar(risk_counts.index.astype(str), risk_counts.values,
            color=sns.color_palette('RdYlGn_r', len(risk_counts)))
axes[0].set_title('Risk Weighting Distribution', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Risk Weighting')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Customer Count')


# Risk vs Income
risk_income = df.groupby('Risk_Weighting')['Estimated_Income'].median().sort_index()
axes[1].bar(risk_income.index.astype(str), risk_income.values,
            color=sns.color_palette('RdYlGn_r', len(risk_income)))
axes[1].set_title('Median Income by Risk Weighting', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Risk Weighting')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Median Income')
axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _:f'${x:,.0f}'))
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig('../reports/Results/13_risk_analysis.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Gender & Nationality Analysis

In [53]:
# Gender Breakdown
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(16,5))


# Pie chart - gender distribution
gender_counts = df['GenderId'].map({1:'Male', 2:'Female'}).value_counts()
axes[0].pie(gender_counts.values, labels=gender_counts.index, autopct='%1.1f%%',
            colors=['#4C72B0', '#DD8452'], startangle = 90)
axes[0].set_title('Gender Distribution', fontweight='bold')

# Median Income by gender
gender_income = df.groupby('GenderId')['Estimated_Income'].median().sort_values(ascending=False)
axes[1].bar(gender_income.index, gender_income.values,
            color=['#4C72B0', '#DD8452'])
axes[1].set_title('Median Income by Gender', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'${x:,.0f}'))

# Top Nationalities
top_nat = df['Nationality'].value_counts().head(10)
axes[2].barh(top_nat.index[::-1], top_nat.values[::-1], color=sns.color_palette('tab10'))
axes[2].set_title('Top 10 Nationalities', fontweight='bold')
axes[2].set_xlabel('Count')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('../reports/Results/14_gender_nationality.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

EDA Summary and Key Insights

In [54]:
# Summary Stats
print('='*81)
print('     Banking EDA - Key Insights Summary')
print('='*81)


print(f"""
📊DATASET
    • Total Customer    : {len(df):,}
    • Toal Columns      : {df.shape[1]}
    • Missing Values    : {df.isnull().sum().sum():,}
    • Duplicate Rows    : {df.duplicated().sum():,}

👤 DEMOGRAPHICS
    • Average Age       : {df['Age'].mean():.1f}years
    • Age Range         : {df['Age'].min():.0f}-{df['Age'].max():.0f}
    • Top Nationality   : {df['Nationality'].mode()[0]}
    • Top Occupation    : {df['Occupation'].mode()[0]}

💰 FINANCIALS
    • Median Income     : ${df['Estimated_Income'].median():,.0f}
    • Median Deposits   : ${df['Bank_Deposits'].median():,.0f}
    • Median Loan bal   : ${df['Bank_Loans'].median():,.0f}
    • Avg Credit Cards  : ${df['Num_Credit_Cards'].mean():,.1f}

🗓️ TENURE
    • Avg Tenure        : {df['Tenure_Years'].mean():.1f} years
    • Max Tenure        : {df['Tenure_Years'].max():.1f} years

⚠️ RISK
    • High Risk (>3)    : {(df['Risk_Weighting']>3).sum():,} customers ({(df['Risk_Weighting']>3).mean()*100:.1f}%)

📈 FEATURED CREATED
    • Total_Assets, Total_Liabilities, Debt_to_Income
    • Account_Diversity, Net_Worth, Tenure_Years, Age_Group


""")

=================================================================================
     Banking EDA - Key Insights Summary
=================================================================================

📊DATASET
    • Total Customer    : 3,000
    • Toal Columns      : 33
    • Missing Values    : 0
    • Duplicate Rows    : 0

👤 DEMOGRAPHICS
    • Average Age       : 51.0years
    • Age Range         : 17-85
    • Top Nationality   : European
    • Top Occupation    : Associate Professor

💰 FINANCIALS
    • Median Income     : $142,313
    • Median Deposits   : $463,316
    • Median Loan bal   : $479,793
    • Avg Credit Cards  : $1.5

🗓️ TENURE
    • Avg Tenure        : 15.9 years
    • Max Tenure        : 31.5 years

⚠️ RISK
    • High Risk (>3)    : 482 customers (16.1%)

📈 FEATURED CREATED
    • Total_Assets, Total_Liabilities, Debt_to_Income
    • Account_Diversity, Net_Worth, Tenure_Years, Age_Group
